name: Cúllar to Oria Loop
name_original: Hasta la población de Oria — ida y vuelta desde Cúllar
description: This moderate 39.8-mile road cycling route from Cúllar to Oria and back offers significant climbs and scenic rural views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Cúllar are set within the Granada Geopark, an area characterized by varied topography including badlands, river valleys, and rolling hills. The region is bordered by the Sierra de Orce and Las Estancias mountain ranges, offering a picturesque backdrop. Cyclists can also explore areas near the Sierra de Baza Natural Park, which features pine-clad slopes, deep valleys, and steep inclines. This diverse terrain provides a range of road cycling opportunities. Best road cycling routes around Cúllar The most popular road cycling route is Route of the Three Reservoirs of the Geoparque, a 67.7 miles (108.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the varied topography of the Granada Geopark. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cúllar to Oria Loop, a moderate 39.8 miles (64.1 km) path. This route features significant climbs and scenic rural vistas, often completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Local road cyclists also love the Cúllar – Baza loop from Benamaurel, a 43.4 miles (69.9 km) trail leading through the diverse landscapes around Baza, often completed in about 3 hours 28 minutes. Road cycling around Cúllar is defined by the varied topography of the Granada Geopark, rolling hills, and the pine-clad slopes of the Sierra de Baza Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging ascents. The routes in Cúllar are highly rated by the komoot community. More than 240 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cúllar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Cúllar?
A: Cúllar is situated within the Granada Geopark, offering a diverse topography for road cyclists. You'll encounter badlands, river valleys, and rolling hills. The region is bordered by the Sierra de Orce and Las Estancias mountain ranges, and routes near the Sierra de Baza Natural Park feature pine-clad slopes, deep valleys, and steep inclines. This variety ensures options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging ascents.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs in Cúllar?
A: Yes, Cúllar offers rewarding climbs for experienced cyclists. The region is known for routes where you can gain around 1,000 meters in elevation for every 50 km, especially away from the valleys. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987061125/route-of-the-three-reservoirs-of-the-geoparque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Three Reservoirs of the Geoparque&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 109 km with over 1,300 meters of elevation gain.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Cúllar?
A: Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally the best times for road cycling in Cúllar. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, making the climbs and descents more enjoyable. Summer can be very hot, while winter might bring colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly road cycling routes in Cúllar?
A: While many routes in Cúllar feature significant climbs, there are options suitable for less experienced cyclists. The area offers a range of difficulties, with some moderate paths available. For a more relaxed experience, the Sierra de Baza Greenway, which follows old railroad tracks, is suitable for gravel and mountain bikes and provides a comfortable ride through natural surroundings, though it's not strictly a road cycling route.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Cúllar offer numerous scenic vistas. You'll traverse the varied landscapes of the Granada Geopark, including badlands and river valleys. The Sierra de Orce and Las Estancias provide picturesque backdrops. For specific viewpoints, consider routes that pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4625289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Piedra Lobera&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive views of the region.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available in Cúllar?
A: Yes, Cúllar offers several excellent loop routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987986627/cullar-to-oria-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cúllar to Oria Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant climbs and scenic rural vistas. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20124266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cúllar – Baza loop from Benamaurel&lt;/a&gt;, leading through diverse landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20124293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cúllar – Galera loop from Cúllar&lt;/a&gt; also provides a great circular experience.

Q: What cultural or historical attractions can I explore near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond cycling, Cúllar is rich in culture and history. You can find unique cave dwellings, and archaeological sites like Cúllar I and the Copper Age town of Malagón. Historic architectural attractions include an Arab military tower and the 16th-century Church of Santa María de la Anunciación. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5899483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galera&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable settlement to explore.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Cúllar area?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes available around Cúllar, catering to various skill levels. These include 1 easy route, 11 moderate routes, and 4 difficult routes, ensuring a suitable option for almost every cyclist.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Cúllar?
A: The road cycling routes in Cúllar are highly regarded by the komoot community. Over 240 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes of the Granada Geopark and the challenging yet rewarding climbs.

Q: Are there any specific natural parks or protected areas that the routes pass through?
A: Many road cycling routes in Cúllar traverse or are near the Sierra de Baza Natural Park. This park is known for its stunning and diverse landscapes, featuring pine-clad slopes, deep valleys, and steep inclines, and is recognized as a 'bioclimatic island' rich in flora and fauna. For more information on the park, you can visit &lt;a href="https://en.wikivoyage.org/wiki/Sierra_de_Baza_Natural_Park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wikivoyage's Sierra de Baza Natural Park page&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some other outdoor activities available in the Cúllar region besides road cycling?
A: Cúllar's mountainous terrain supports a variety of outdoor activities. Besides road cycling, you can enjoy hiking, climbing, and paragliding. The Sierra de Baza Natural Park offers forest tracks ideal for mountain biking. The ecological richness of the area also makes it a great spot for mushroom picking and birdwatching.

Q: Are there any routes that follow old railway lines?
A: While primarily focused on road cycling, the region does offer the Sierra de Baza Greenway, which follows old railroad tracks. This route is particularly suitable for gravel and mountain bikes, providing a comfortable ride through natural surroundings, and could be an alternative for cyclists looking for a different experience.
tips: Town located at 897 meters of altitude in the province of Granada. The sites indicate possible hominid activities, as well as occupation in Roman times. It is not until the Muslim presence when a permanent settlement is found in the current nucleus.
The Basilica of Nuestra Señora de las Mercedes is a late Baroque jewel in the heart of Almería. Its imposing presence in the modest historic center of Oria makes it a must-see.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this route in Cúllar?
faqs.answer: While specific parking facilities for this exact route aren't detailed, Cúllar is a town with various public parking options. You should be able to find suitable street parking or designated areas within the town to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region around Cúllar and Oria generally offers good cycling conditions during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). These seasons typically provide milder temperatures, making the significant climbs and descents more enjoyable. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures and occasional rain.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this racebike route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It primarily uses public roads and is freely accessible.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty, a distance of over 64 km, and an elevation gain of nearly 800 meters, this route is best suited for cyclists with at least a good level of fitness and some experience with road cycling. Beginners might find the sustained climbs and overall distance challenging.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 64 km racebike route is approximately 2 hours and 57 minutes. This is an average estimate and can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and any stops you make along the way.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect mostly paved roads, suitable for a racebike. However, be prepared for significant elevation changes, including both challenging climbs and descents, as is typical for cycling in this region. The route offers varied terrain that will test your endurance.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in the town of Cúllar. A notable highlight on the route is the opportunity to visit the Basilica of Our Lady of Mercy of Oria in the town of Oria, which serves as the turnaround point for this round trip.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or rest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Cúllar and Oria are towns where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel and rest. It's advisable to carry enough water and snacks for the sections between these towns, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: For this racebike tour, you should bring your road bike, a helmet, appropriate cycling apparel, plenty of water, energy snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and navigation tools. Given the elevation changes, consider gearing suitable for climbing.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a racebike route primarily on public roads, it is generally not recommended for cycling with dogs. The speeds and traffic conditions are not suitable for pets, and there are no specific provisions for dogs along the route.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly intersect with other paths. You will encounter parts of the 'Cúllar histórica y geológica', 'Sauco - El Madroñal', 'El Camino Espiritual del Sur', and 'Colada del Camino del Saúco' trails, among others.

waypoints.name: Basilica of Our Lady of Mercy of Oria
